The '''MB-C1 medium transport''', also known as the '''Mobquet Medium Transport''' or '''Mobquet Medium Cargo Hauler''', was a transport ship produced by [[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet Swoops and Speeders]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Starlight-Intruder_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Starlight Intruder [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]] (a modified Mobquet medium transport).]]
Although one of the slower transports, its slightly reinforced hull and extra space helped increase its popularity.<ref name="FF133" />

The ship consisted of three modular nodes, that could be modified to serve the need of the owner. The standard design included crew quarters, storage, defensive weapons in front node, speeder racks in the second node and computer units in the third node. It wasn't hard to install new weapons, increase the passenger capacity or modify the cargo space.<ref name="RECG" />

Common installed weapons were two fire-linked twin medium laser cannons (which were also retractable) and a concussion missile launcher that was dorsal mounted.<ref name="SWEotERPGBeta">[[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Beta|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Beta]]</ref>

==History==
The ship was one the few Mobquet attempts to break into the starship market.<ref name="FF133" /> Because of Tagge's connection with the Empire, the freighter was licensed as medium transport rather than a heavy cargo carrier.<ref name="RECG" />

At least one Mobquet medium transport was used to make up a [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Jedi Temple]].<ref name="SWL25">[[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]]</ref> A Mobquet medium transport was also the basis for the ''[[Starlight Intruder]]''{{'s}} design.<ref name="FF133" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Starlight-Intruder--as-seen-in-Firefly.jpg|thumb|right|180px|A Mobquet Medium Transport as seen in [[Wikipedia:Firefly (TV series)|''Firefly'']].]]

The Mobquet Medium Transport is shown in the background of the episodes [[Wikipedia:Serenity (Firefly episode)|''Serenity'']], [[Wikipedia:Shindig (Firefly episode)|''Shindig'']], and [[Wikipedia:War Stories (Firefly episode)|''War Stories'']] of the show [[Wikipedia:Firefly (TV series)|''Firefly'']]. In response, [[Tom Hodges|Thomas Hodges]], artist of the webcomic ''[[Evasive Action: Prey]]'', included the title ship of ''Firefly'' in the comic. See [[Firefly-class mid-bulk transport|''Firefly''-class mid-bulk transport]].

The Mobquet transports in [[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]] have textures based on the [[Starlight Intruder]], including a shape on top of the second cargo module which is actually a replica of the Millennium Falcon.

Typical specifications vary in many sources, i.e. in ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'' it doesn't carry a missile launcher and the length is only 79.3 meters. The armament varies from source to source (often with the statistics of modified examples given as the actual ship's) although the most recent source indicates a laser cannon and a concussion missile launcher, it goes on to state it can be modified with considerable room for added weapon systems.''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]'''

The typical crew stated in ''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]'' is the skeleton crew for [[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]; passengers, hyperdrive class and the ship's cost also vary.

It was first identified as MB-C1 medium transport in the [[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Beta|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Beta]].<ref name="SWEotERPGBeta" />
